Configure the WRD and Create a wrd.war File on iSeries

The IBM Integrated Web application server for i can deploy applications that are in the form of a web archive file, .war file or a web application bundle .wab file. The Uniface Web Request Dispatcher (WRD) servlet is not in this format, so it must be packaged into a .war file.

You can do this on your iSeries machine under Q-shell, or on a Windows machine on which a Java 2 SDK 1.4 or higher is installed.

  1. Edit the web.xml file that defines the Uniface servlets.
    1. Use WRKLNK to navigate to this directory: IFS-installation-directory/uniface/webapps/uniface/WEB-INF. Edit the file web.xml with option 2 (you might need to remove the Read-only attribute first).
      You can also edit this file on the iSeries using the CL command EDTF.
    2. In web.xml, edit all three occurrences of the UV8: connection string, specifying the correct host identifier, port number, user name and password, and UST. Other configuration settings can also be specified. 
      <param-value>UV8:localhost+13001|ChangeThisUser|ChangeThisPassword|wasv</param-value>
      • UV8:—introduces the WRD connection string; do not change it.
      • localhost+13001—identifies the UROUTER that will receive WRD requests. For a local Uniface installation, you can use localhost as the host identifier.

        If you do not know which port number your iSeries UROUTER is listening to, use WRKACTJOB option 5, option 10 to display its job log; the top line shows how the UROUTER was invoked and what its port number is.

      • | ChangeThisUser | ChangeThisPassword—user name and password of an authorized Uniface Server user, typically UNIFSID. The pipe symbols (|) are required separators.
      • | UST—a valid UST that the Uniface Router can translate to a Uniface Server invocation, that is, one that has an entry in the Uniface Router assignment file urouter.asn, in the [SERVERS] section. The Uniface default installation has a wasv entry which usually does not need to be changed. The pipe symbol separator is required.
      Tip: After installation the TESTABLE parameter is set to true in web.xml. This setting makes it possible to display a test page to confirm that the servlet is running and can connect to Uniface. Once this is confirmed, it is recommended that it be removed or set to false. For more information, see TESTABLE.
  2. Package directories in IFS-installation-directory/uniface/webapps/uniface into the wrd.war file using the following command (in Q-shell):

    cd IFS-installation-directory/uniface/webapps/uniface jar -cf wrd.war *

    You can also do this on a Windows workstation in a command prompt. In that case you must transfer the wrd.war file to the target iSeries, either by binary FTP, or by drag-and-drop into a shared IFS directory.

Related Links
  • Configuration Settings: Uniface Servlet

    Uniface servlets run in the environment of the web application. Settings for the Uniface servlets, the WRD and SRD, are stored in the web.xml file, which is the configuration file of the web application.
